sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Center sub-cell (2,0) is not clickable either. The center cell is definitely not clickable. So the center must be automatically updated based on the outer grid configuration, OR the center is not part of the puzzle at all.
Let me reconsider the problem. Maybe the transformation rule is different. Let me look at the top-left and top-right more carefully:
Top-left outer: b R b / R . b / b R R
Top-right outer: b R b / R . R / b R b
Top-left center: g W g / W R g / g W W
Top-right center: g W g / W R W / g W g
The changes from top-left to top-right:
Outer: (1,2) b->R, (2,2) R->b
Center: (1,2) g->W, (2,2) W->g
So the rule is "swap (1,2) and (2,2)" for both outer and center.
Now for the bottom pair:
Bottom-left outer: R b b / R . R / b b R
Bottom-left center: W g g / W R W / g g W
Applying "swap (1,2) and (2,2)":
Outer: (1,2)=R, (2,2)=R → no change → target outer: R b b / R . R / b b R
Center: (1,2)=W, (2,2)=W → no change → target center: W g g / W R W / g g W
So the target bottom-right should be:
Outer: R b b / R . R / b b R
Center: W g g / W R W / g g W
Current bottom-right:
Outer: R b b / R . R / b b R ✓
Center: W g g / W R W / W g g ✗
The outer matches but the center doesn't. And the center is not clickable.
Hmm, maybe the center DOES auto-update but only when the outer grid is in a specific configuration. Let me try different outer configurations to see if the center changes.
Actually, wait. Let me reconsider the initial state. The initial bottom-right outer was all b, and the initial bottom-right center was W g g / W R W / W g g. Maybe the center is NOT automatically updated, and the initial center is the "default" center.
But then how do I change the center? The center is not clickable...
Unless... the center is part of the outer grid and I need to click on the outer grid cells to change the center? That doesn't make sense.
Let me try a completely different hypothesis. What if the goal is NOT to match the bottom-left, but to match the top-right?
Top-right outer: b R b / R . R / b R b
Top-right center: g W g / W R W / g W g
If I set the bottom-right to match the top-right:
Outer: b R b / R . R / b R b
Center: g W g / W R W / g W g
But the center is not clickable, so this doesn't help.
OK, let me try yet another hypothesis. What if the center cell is NOT part of the puzzle, and the level is complete when the outer grid matches the target?
Current outer: R b b / R . R / b b R
This matches the bottom-left outer. But the level is not complete. So maybe the target is not the bottom-left outer.
Let me try setting the outer grid to match the top-right outer: b R b / R . R / b R b.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Center cell is definitely not clickable. Outer grid matches bottom-left but level not complete. Maybe the target outer is different. Let me try matching the top-right outer: b R b / R . R / b R b.
Plan: Set outer grid to match top-right: b R b / R . R / b R b.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current outer: R b b / R . R / b b R
# Target outer (top-right): b R b / R . R / b R b
# Need: (0,0) R->b, (0,1) b->R, (2,1) b->R, (2,2) R->b

# Batch: change (0,0), (0,1), (2,1), (2,2)
predict(expect_change=True, note='Set outer to match top-right: b R b / R . R / b R b')
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 38},  # (0,0) R->b
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 46},  # (0,1) b->R
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 46},  # (2,1) b->R
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 54},  # (2,2) R->b
])

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("After setting outer to top-right:")
for r in [38, 46, 54]:
    c0 = lines[r][36:42]
    c1 = lines[r][44:50]
    c2 = lines[r][52:58]
    print(f"  {r}: {c0} {c1} {c2}")
print("Center:")
for r in range(44, 50):
    print(f"  {r}: '{lines[r][44:50]}'")
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed', False))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
